description: MCP server authentication subsystem supporting multiple auth strategies. Includes standard OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow with PKCE, RFC 8693 cross-app access (XAA) token exchange for silent auth, enterprise IdP login for acquiring OIDC id_tokens, OAuth callback port management, and elicitation handling for interactive auth prompts during server connections.

nodes.name: OAuthFlow
nodes.description: Core OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow implementation and MCP auth provider. Contains the ClaudeAuthProvider class (OAuthClientProvider implementation with secure storage, token persistence, DCR, refresh with transient-error retry, and XAA-aware refresh fallback), the performMCPOAuthFlow orchestrator (browser-based PKCE flow with local callback server, cancellation, timeout, step-up auth detection), performMCPXaaAuth (silent XAA auth with fallback to browser OAuth on failure), token revocation (RFC 7009 with client_secret_basic/post and Bearer fallback), OAuth error normalization for non-compliant servers (e.g. Slack), auth server metadata discovery (RFC 9728 → RFC 8414 with path-aware fallback), client secret management helpers, and analytics instrumentation for auth events.

edges.description: performMCPXaaAuth invokes performCrossAppAccess to execute the silent RFC 8693/7523 token exchange chain, and ClaudeAuthProvider.xaaRefresh uses it for token refresh

performMCPXaaAuth calls acquireIdpIdToken to obtain the OIDC id_token needed for XAA, reads cached tokens via getCachedIdpIdToken, clears them on exchange failure via clearIdpIdToken, and uses discoverOidc for IdP metadata
Uses buildRedirectUri and findAvailablePort to set up the local HTTP callback server for the OAuth authorization code redirect

nodes.name: XAATokenExchange
nodes.description: RFC 8693 Cross-App Access (XAA) token exchange protocol implementation. Structured as four Layer-2 operations (PRM discovery via RFC 9728, AS metadata discovery via RFC 8414 with issuer-mismatch and HTTPS validation, RFC 8693 token exchange at IdP to obtain an ID-JAG, RFC 7523 JWT bearer grant at the AS to obtain an access_token) plus one Layer-3 orchestrator (performCrossAppAccess) that composes them. Includes XaaTokenExchangeError with shouldClearIdToken semantics for cache invalidation decisions, Zod response schemas with coercion for non-conformant IdPs, sensitive token redaction for debug logging, and abort signal propagation.

nodes.name: IdPLogin
nodes.description: Enterprise IdP OIDC login flow for acquiring and caching id_tokens used by XAA. Implements the full authorization_code + PKCE browser flow against an enterprise IdP (one browser pop → N silent MCP server auths). Manages id_token caching in secure storage keyed by normalized IdP issuer URL, with JWT exp-based TTL and expiry buffer. Includes OIDC discovery with path-append (not replace) for Azure AD/Okta/Keycloak compatibility, IdP client secret storage, local HTTP callback server with state/CSRF validation and XSS-sanitized error pages, configurable callback port, abort signal support, and the isXaaEnabled feature gate.

edges.description: Uses buildRedirectUri and findAvailablePort to configure the local callback server for the IdP authorization code redirect

nodes.name: OAuthPort
nodes.description: OAuth redirect port management utilities shared by the main OAuth flow and IdP login. Provides findAvailablePort (random port selection within platform-specific ranges with configurable override via MCP_OAUTH_CALLBACK_PORT, fallback to port 3118) and buildRedirectUri (constructs http://localhost:{port}/callback per RFC 8252 Section 7.3 for native app loopback redirects). Extracted to break the auth.ts ↔ xaaIdpLogin.ts circular dependency.

nodes.name: ElicitationHandler
nodes.description: MCP elicitation request/response handler for interactive auth prompts during server connections. Registers JSON-RPC request and notification handlers on the MCP Client for form-mode and URL-mode elicitations. Manages an elicitation queue in React app state, supports abort signal cancellation, runs pre-elicitation and post-response hooks (which can programmatically resolve or block elicitations), handles completion notifications for URL-mode flows, and logs elicitation analytics events.
